title: Pagico vs Things 3 for Power users
meta_description: Pagico vs Things 3 for power users who need tasks linked natively to files, contacts, and related resources.
constraint_lens: You need tasks, files, and contacts connected inside one system without hitting structural limits.

one_second_verdict.summary: Best for power users who manage tasks, files, and contacts together.
one_second_verdict.reason: Things 3 fails first because task management cannot link natively to files and contacts within one model.

verdict.summary: Pagico wins for power users who manage tasks, files, and contacts together. It uses a unified model where projects can contain tasks, attached files, and linked contacts. Things 3 focuses on tasks with areas and projects but does not natively link files and contacts as first-class entities. If task management cannot link natively to files and contacts within one model, Things 3 fails first.
verdict.decision_rule: If task management cannot link natively to files and contacts within one model, Things 3 fails first.

sections.heading: Best fit for cross-entity task systems
sections.content: You manage tasks, files, and contacts together and expect cross-entity linking. Pagico allows you to attach documents and connect contacts directly inside projects. Things 3 organizes tasks into projects and areas but treats files and contacts as external references.

bullets.point: Unified model for tasks, files, and contacts
bullets.why_it_matters: You store related resources inside the same project rather than linking out to separate apps.
You associate specific people directly with tasks and documents.
You visualize dependencies and project flow beyond simple task lists.
You manage to-dos with minimal distraction from non-task entities.
You structure responsibilities without configuring complex project models.
You capture tasks rapidly without opening additional panels.

sections.heading: Where each tool can break down

items.fails_when: You only want lightweight task lists without managing files and contacts inside the app.
items.what_to_do_instead: Use Things 3 for a focused task-only workflow.

items.fails_when: You need tasks linked directly to project files and specific contacts within one system.
items.what_to_do_instead: Switch to Pagico for integrated resource management.

sections.heading: When this verdict might flip
sections.content: If you keep files in cloud storage and contacts in a separate CRM and only need clean task tracking, Things 3 may feel sufficient.

sections.heading: Quick rules
rules: If you need tasks, files, and contacts linked natively, choose Pagico.
If you prefer a clean task-only app, consider Things 3.
If cross-entity linking matters long term, avoid task-only models.

faqs.q: Does Things 3 support linking contacts inside projects?
faqs.a: It focuses on tasks and does not treat contacts as first-class linked entities.
Yes. Files can be attached directly within project spaces.

faqs.q: Which has the higher ceiling for integrated work management?
faqs.a: Pagico generally has the higher ceiling due to its unified entity model.

faqs.q: Which is simpler for pure task tracking?
faqs.a: Things 3 is simpler if you only need structured to-do lists.
